title: Pump P-200 — General Specifications
text: The P-200 is a centrifugal process pump used for water and light-fluid transfer. Nominal flow rate is 40 m3/h. The maximum operating pressure is 16 bar. Inlet size is DN65 and outlet size is DN50. Operating temperature range is 5 to 90 degrees Celsius. The pump uses a mechanical seal and requires flooded suction.

title: Pump P-200 — Maintenance Schedule
text: Preventive maintenance for the P-200 pump is due every 2000 operating hours. Inspect the mechanical seal, check bearing play, and verify alignment. Do not exceed the rated pressure; for this unit the maximum operating pressure is 12 bar. Replace the seal kit if leakage is observed. Record vibration readings during each service.

title: Compressor C-100 — Specifications
text: The C-100 is a rotary screw air compressor. Rated output is 6 m3/min at 8 bar. Motor power is 37 kW. It includes an integrated air-oil separator and an after-cooler. Ambient operating temperature should stay below 40 degrees Celsius. Condensate must be drained daily.

title: Motor M-50 — Startup Procedure
text: Before starting the M-50 electric motor, confirm the isolation switch is off, verify insulation resistance, and ensure the coupling guard is fitted. Start in the no-load condition first. Check the direction of rotation against the arrow on the housing. Monitor current draw during the first minute; it must remain within the nameplate rating.

title: Fan F-30 — Vibration Limits
text: For F-30 axial fan units, the acceptable vibration velocity limit is 4.5 mm/s RMS measured at the bearing housing. Readings above this level indicate imbalance or bearing wear and require investigation. Measure in three axes during routine checks.

title: Vibration Note — F-30 Units
text: On F-30 fans, keep the vibration velocity at or below 4.5 mm/s RMS at the bearing. If the value rises above the limit, inspect for imbalance or a worn bearing. Take measurements along three axes at each inspection.

title: Error Codes — Group A
text: Code E-207 indicates an overpressure trip on pump units. When E-207 appears, stop the pump, inspect the pressure relief valve, and check for a blocked discharge line before restarting. Code E-208 indicates a dry-run condition. Reset only after the cause is cleared.

title: Error Codes — Group B
text: Code E-115 indicates a sensor fault, usually an open circuit on a temperature probe. Check wiring and the probe connector. Code E-120 indicates a communication timeout between the controller and a field device. Verify the bus wiring and termination.

title: Bearing BRG-4410 — Replacement
text: The BRG-4410 is a sealed deep-groove ball bearing used on several fan and motor assemblies. Its recommended replacement interval is 12000 operating hours or when vibration exceeds the unit limit, whichever comes first. Use the correct puller and do not reuse a bearing once removed.

title: Temperature Sensor — Calibration
text: Calibrate temperature sensors every 12 months. Use a reference thermometer at two points, near the low and high ends of the operating range. Adjust the offset and span so the reading matches the reference within tolerance. Log the as-found and as-left values.

title: Lockout / Tagout Procedure
text: Before any maintenance, isolate all energy sources, apply a personal lock and tag, and verify zero energy. Only the person who applied a lock may remove it. Test the start control to confirm the equipment cannot operate. Restore energy only after all guards are refitted.

title: Preventive Maintenance Intervals
text: General intervals: inspect belts and couplings monthly; lubricate applicable bearings per the equipment sheet; check instrument calibration yearly. Pumps are serviced every 2000 hours and compressors every 4000 hours. Keep a record of every intervention with date and technician.

title: Commissioning Checklist
text: During commissioning, verify nameplate data, confirm correct rotation, check alignment and vibration at first run, and record baseline readings. Confirm all protection settings and interlocks operate. Hand over the baseline record to the maintenance owner.

title: Emergency Shutdown Steps
text: In an emergency, press the nearest emergency stop, isolate the main supply, and notify the shift supervisor. Do not attempt repairs while the line is energized. After a shutdown, inspect for damage and record the event before any restart.

title: Spare Parts — Ordering Process
text: To order spare parts, identify the part number from the equipment sheet, check stock, and raise a request with the quantity and required date. Critical spares should be held in minimum stock. Record the part number and supplier for traceability.

title: Sensor Data Logging
text: Field sensor readings are logged at fixed intervals with a timestamp and a device identifier. Store readings so that duplicates from a retried message are not counted twice. Keep raw values and any unit of measure. Review the log for gaps after a network interruption.
